steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Text Structure and Purpose. The question asks for the overall structure of the poem, so identify what the speaker does in the opening lines and what he does in the closing lines.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The speaker questions an increasingly prevalent attitude, then summarizes his worldview.: The speaker does not question an attitude or describe it as increasingly prevalent; he addresses a charge made against him specifically. He also ends with a future goal, not a worldview summary. ✗
  • Choice B — The speaker regrets his isolation from others, then predicts a profound change in society.: The speaker expresses no regret and no isolation. He ends by announcing an ambition he intends to establish, not a prediction that society will change on its own. ✗
  • Choice C — The speaker concedes his personal shortcomings, then boasts of his many achievements.: The speaker concedes nothing; he refutes the charge. He also lists no past achievements — only a single future goal. ✗
  • Choice D — The speaker addresses a criticism leveled against him, then announces a grand ambition of his.: The opening “I HEAR it is charged against me” is a direct response to a criticism. After dismissing it, the speaker announces the grand goal of establishing “the institution of the dear love of comrades.” Both halves match precisely. ✓
That matches Choice D — The speaker addresses a criticism leveled against him, then announces a grand ambition of his..
